entities.name: ACACIA DECURRENS
entities.description: A tree species used in agroforestry systems to remediate acid soils and generate income through charcoal making

entities.name: AGROFORESTRY SYSTEM
entities.description: The integration of Acacia decurrens into landscapes to improve soil properties and livelihoods

entities.name: SOIL ACIDITY
entities.description: A critical challenge for crop production in the Ethiopian highlands characterized by low soil pH

entities.name: ETHIOPIAN HIGHLANDS
entities.description: The geographic region where land degradation and soil acidity are significant challenges

entities.name: LIMING
entities.description: An expensive practice used to remediate soil acidity

entities.name: CHARCOAL MAKING
entities.description: A process of producing charcoal that generates income and creates kiln sites which affect soil properties

entities.name: SOIL PH
entities.description: A measure of soil acidity that is increased by Acacia decurrens plantations and charcoal-making kiln sites

entities.name: SOIL ORGANIC CARBON
entities.description: A soil property (SOC) that increases under Acacia decurrens plantations and charcoal-making kiln sites

entities.name: CATION EXCHANGE CAPACITY
entities.description: A soil property (CEC) improved by the integration of Acacia decurrens-based agroforestry

entities.name: AVAILABLE BRAY PHOSPHORUS
entities.description: A soil property (Bray-P) increased by charcoal-making kiln spots and strongly correlated with soil pH

entities.name: LAND DEGRADATION
entities.description: The deterioration of land quality, associated with soil acidity in the Ethiopian highlands

entities.name: CROP PRODUCTION
entities.description: The process of growing crops, which is challenged by soil acidity

entities.name: CARBON SEQUESTRATION
entities.description: The process of capturing and storing atmospheric carbon, improved by agroforestry practices

entities.name: FARMERS' INCOME
entities.description: Financial gain generated by farmers through charcoal making from Acacia decurrens
